free_text: Charge Distribution in the Fission of U-238.
Last checking has been done.
DATE is corrected
Uranium Foils of 1-Ml Thickness with an Isotopic
Content of 99.6% U-238 and 0.4% Th-232.The Foils were
Cut Into Circular Discs of 1.84-cm Diam. And
Sandwiched Between Aluminum Catcher Foils.
. With Gaussian Charge Dispersion.
.Details of
Experiments.
Mass
Yield Curves For
For 238-U(D,F)
- By Author - In Order to Convert Measured Cross
Sections to Fractional Chain Yields It was Necessary to
Construct Mass Yield Curve For the Systems of Interest.
Choppin and Meyer Reported the Mass Chain Yield For
238-U Bombarded with Protons of 10 and 12 MeV Incident
Energies.  In the Course of this Work the Cumulative
Chain Yields For Several Mass Chains (83, 93, 99, 111,
113, 131, 134, 138) Were Determined.  Combining the
Data from these Two Sources It was Possible to
Construct Very Reliable Mass Chain Yield Curves For 9.5
And 11.3 Proton Fission of 238-U.
For the Deutron System Mass Yield Data from Sugihara'S
Paper.
.The Uncertainty is Reported by Authors.
The Errors Listed are Those Arising from Deviations
Between Replicate Determinations and Should not be
Considered As Absolute Errors.In View of the Low Cross
Section For Formation of these Isotopes and the
Resulting Poor Counting Statistics It is Felt That the
Absolute Error is on the Order of 20 %.
Noany Analysis is Presented.
